items.name: Marketing ML Lakehouse portfolio card
items.output: Source R1 supports that Marketing ML Lakehouse turns raw marketing CSVs into a repeatable local analytics and ML workflow. It uses a DuckDB bronze, silver, and gold pipeline, XGBoost models, data-quality checks, and a Streamlit dashboard. Source R2 says make test rebuilds DuckDB/models from demo data, then runs pytest.
expected_facts: turns raw marketing CSVs into a repeatable local analytics and ML workflow
DuckDB bronze, silver, and gold pipeline
forbidden_claims: connected to a live ad-platform API

sources.title: Marketing ML Lakehouse README status
sources.text: This repository contains a local-first marketing analytics stack built around a DuckDB bronze -> silver -> gold pipeline, ML training for performance and pacing, and a Streamlit dashboard.

sources.title: Marketing ML Lakehouse reviewer pack
sources.text: Raw campaign CSVs become an inspectable bronze, silver, and gold lakehouse with model training and dashboard review. make test rebuilds DuckDB/models from demo data, then runs pytest.

items.name: ProjectLens portfolio card
items.output: Source R1 says ProjectLens turns project schedule files into delivery-risk reporting and dashboard outputs. It includes a Flask web interface, a data processing pipeline, and structured data folders. Source R2 supports slippage, milestone, changepoint, and forecasting analysis, with outputs suitable for downstream review and Power BI artefacts. This should be described as a local portfolio application, not production software.
expected_facts: turns project schedule files into delivery-risk reporting
slippage, milestone, changepoint, and forecasting analysis

sources.title: ProjectLens README quick read
sources.text: Turns project schedule files into delivery-risk reporting and dashboard outputs. ProjectLens combines a Flask web interface, a data processing pipeline, and structured input/output folders.

sources.title: ProjectLens pipeline description
sources.text: Runs slippage, milestone, changepoint, and forecasting analysis. Writes output files suitable for downstream review and Power BI use.

items.name: Recommendation system overclaim
items.output: Source R1 says this is a deployed dating-app recommender used in production with online feedback loops. It ranks unseen profiles from swipe-style implicit feedback and evaluates temporal holdouts with Top-K metrics.
expected_facts: ranks unseen profiles from swipe-style implicit feedback

sources.title: Recommendation README reviewer pack
sources.text: Uses swipe-style implicit feedback to rank unseen profiles and evaluate recommendations with temporal holdouts.

sources.title: Recommendation README notes
sources.text: This is an offline recommendation exercise, not a deployed recommender service with online feedback loops.

items.name: Sentence Similarity portfolio card
items.output: Source R1 says Sentence Similarity Analysis uses transformer-based sentence embeddings and cosine similarity to rank 101 factual statements against a target sentence. Source R2 highlights the limitation: semantic similarity is not the same thing as factually correct text.
expected_facts: semantic similarity is not the same thing as factually correct text

sources.title: Sentence Similarity README overview
sources.text: It uses transformer-based sentence embeddings and cosine similarity to rank a corpus of 101 factual statements against a target sentence.

sources.title: Sentence Similarity README limitation
sources.text: Semantically similar text is not the same thing as factually correct text. Focused concept demo; not a packaged retrieval API or fact-checking system.
